source.name: Michael Genesereth
source.notes_excerpt: Michael Genesereth (born 1948) is an American logician and computer scientist, who is most known for his work on computational logic and applications of that work in enterprise management, computational law, and general game playing. Genesereth is professor in the Computer Science Department at Stanford University and a professor by courtesy in the Stanford Law School. His 1987 textbook on Logical

target.name: Stuart Russell
target.notes_excerpt: Stuart Russell is a professor of computer science at the University of California, Berkeley and holds the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering. He co-authored the textbook 'Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach' with Peter Norvig, which is used in over 1,500 universities in 135 countries. source.name: Chuck Schumer
source.notes_excerpt: Chuck Schumer is a Democratic U.S. Senator from New York who has served since 1999 and is currently Senate Majority Leader. He led a bipartisan working group that released a 31-page AI policy roadmap in May 2024, recommending at least $32 billion in government spending to accelerate AI research and development. source.name: Aaron Courville

evidence: Doctoral advisor: Michael J. Berry, William Bialek

source.name: Michael J. Berry
source.notes_excerpt: Professor at Princeton University, Department of Molecular Biology and Princeton Neuroscience Institute. Runs the Berry Lab studying neural coding in the retina. Co-advised Dario Amodei (Anthropic CEO) during doctoral research on large-scale electrophysiology of neural circuits. Published extensively on neural population coding and information processing in biological systems.

target.name: Dario Amodei
target.notes_excerpt: Dario Amodei (born 1983) is an American artificial intelligence researcher and entrepreneur who co-founded Anthropic in 2021 with his sister Daniela Amodei. He previously served as vice president of research at OpenAI, where he led the development of large language models like GPT-2 and GPT-3. source.name: Joseph Stiglitz
source.notes_excerpt: Warns of AI bubble that could burst while simultaneously displacing workers, creating worst-case scenario without proper retraining infrastructure. Distinguishes between 'AI' (replacement) and 'IA' (intelligence assisting), advocating for latter approach. Signed Global Call for AI Red Lines in 2026. Predicts AI will primarily impact routine white-collar jobs while augmenting rather than replacing

evidence: In September 2023, Christiano was appointed to the UK government's Frontier AI Taskforce advisory board

source.name: Paul Christiano
source.notes_excerpt: Paul Christiano is Head of AI Safety for the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute at NIST, where he designs and conducts tests of frontier AI models focusing on capabilities of national security concern. He founded the Alignment Research Center, a nonprofit research organization that seeks to align future machine learning systems with human interests. He previously ran the language model

target.name: UK government's Frontier AI Taskforce
target.notes_excerpt: UK government body established April 2023 with £100m mandate to evaluate frontier AI safety risks ahead of the Bletchley Park AI Safety Summit. Chaired by Ian Hogarth, it transitioned in November 2023 into the AI Safety Institute (later renamed AI Security Institute in 2025), which is a separate entity in this database.

evidence: He is the chief executive officer and co-founder of Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs, and a UK Government AI Adviser

source.name: Demis Hassabis
source.notes_excerpt: Demis Hassabis is the CEO and co-founder of Google DeepMind, an artificial intelligence company that was founded in London in 2010 and acquired by Google in 2014. He shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry with John M. Jumper for their work on AlphaFold, an AI tool that predicts protein structures. He is also the founder and CEO of Isomorphic Labs, a biotechnology company focused on drug discover

Chaired by Ian Hogarth, it transitioned in November 2023 into the AI Safety Institute (later renamed AI Security Institute in 2025), which is a separate entity in this database.

evidence: Gebru was advised during her PhD program by Fei-Fei Li

source.name: Fei-Fei Li
source.notes_excerpt: Fei-Fei Li is a Chinese-born American computer scientist best known for establishing ImageNet, the dataset that enabled rapid advances in computer vision in the 2010s. She is a professor of computer science at Stanford University, with research expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, computer vision, and cognitive neuroscience. She has been called the godmother of AI

target.name: Timnit Gebru
target.notes_excerpt: Timnit Gebru is the founder and executive director of The Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research (DAIR) Institute. She was co-lead of the Ethical AI research team at Google until her firing in 2020 (along with her co-lead) for writing a paper warning about the dangers of large language models and raising issues of discrimination in the workplace.
